rationale: The sole substantive ground-truth concern (yJader's comment) is that already_submitted shouldn't gate the unwrap because both submit_initial and submit_update reject list prompts identically. X explicitly verifies exactly this (stage_pool.py:951-955 and 1022-1026), reproducing the reviewer's core reasoning almost precisely. Y identifies the same line and calls the fix correct but never checks that both submit paths reject lists, missing the semantic 'why' that is the entire content of the ground-truth comment. Y also inflates a non-issue into a [major] blocking-style test request despite two clean LGTM approvals, and adds a docstring/hasattr claim that can't be grounded in the given diff. Both cite an issue number not present in the diff (X: #4079, Y: #4839), a wash on that front. X is more tightly grounded and mirrors the actual reviewer insight, giving it a clear edge.
